description:
GOAL: Wire VecF64 element storage, the bifurcation of ~v[...] sigil into VecI64 vs VecF64 at lower-time, and end-to-end JIT support for f64-element vector literals.

## Why deferred from .11.14

The .11.14 implementation chose option (a): land VecI64 / VecU8 / VecBit elegantly now, defer VecF64 until boxed-float codegen lands. F64 element storage requires Const::Float codegen (currently gated to '.11.10+' placeholder error), which is fz-ul4.11.20 (boxed floats in the JIT).

## Decisions to inherit from .11.14

- Heap layout: HeapHeader (16) + len:u32 (4) + padding (4) + raw_payload.
- VecKindIr::F64 should already be a variant after .11.14 (added explicitly during the I64/U8/Bit bifurcation).
- alloc_vec_f64 is straightforward: 8-byte payload per element.

## Deliverables

- Lower-time bifurcation in lower_vec_lit: when VecKind::Numeric and any element is Expr::Float, emit MakeVec(F64, ...). Otherwise I64. (Mixed int/float should error or coerce to F64 — pick during impl.)
- alloc_vec_f64 in Heap.
- TLS-staged push for f64 element bits in fz_vec_push.
- render_vec arm for VecF64.
- vec_get(vec_f64, i) returns boxed f64 (via .11.20's float boxing).

## Tests

- ~v[1.0, 2.0, 3.0] allocates a VecF64 with 3 elements.
- vec_get(~v[1.0, 2.0, 3.0], 1) returns 2.0.
- print(~v[1.0, 2.0]) renders as ~v[1.0, 2.0].
- Mixed ~v[1, 2.0] rule: pick coerce or error during impl, document.

## Dependencies

- BLOCKED BY: fz-ul4.11.20 (Boxed floats in the JIT).
- BLOCKED BY: fz-ul4.11.14 (Vec storage layout + bifurcation skeleton).

## Out of scope

- vec_map / vec_reduce on VecF64 (handled in .11.22 once both .11.14 and this land).
title: VecF64 storage and ~v[1.0, 2.0] sigil
